title: Senior Executive, Korea Market
description:
Job Description:

1. Monthly Closing and Premium Booking Governance

  • Review post-bind insurance slip to ensure completeness and accuracy (e.g. slip/period/terms and conditions/premium as agreed) prior to processing.
  • Summarize and verify each risk in the prescribed format, and issue clear and accurate processing instructions to the Processing Admin team for policy issuance.
  • Validate that system booking is completed correctly in line with processing instructions and underwriting intent.
  • Monitor premium booking progress throughout the month and provide real-time updates to support month-end closing, including maintaining the monthly booking list on a real-time basis.

2. Underwriting Support and Renewal Execution   

  • Consolidate underwriting information received from intermediaries and structure data for underwriting review and decision-making.
  • Prepare pricing model outputs to assess rate adequacy and support underwriting recommendations, and present key findings to the underwriters.
  • Draft slips and supporting documents based on underwriter direction and coordinate placement/queries with intermediaries.
  • Support renewal preparation, including risk selection inputs and ensuring required information is captured and documented.
  • Send renewal reminders to intermediaries and closely follow up on marine renewals through binding.

3. Reporting, Analytics, and Management Information

  • Extract premium and claims data from internal systems; validate data integrity and reconcile variances prior to reporting.
  • Prepare analysis and presentation materials for monthly/annual reporting and portfolio review.
  • Produce recurring management reports, including but not limited to:        
  • - Contract Certainty Report            
  • - GWP Variance Analysis Report           
  • - Renewal Updates and Effective Rate Movement Report            
  • - Data analysis and presentation materials for the monthly Business and Operations Development Meeting / Quarterly Portfolio Review.

4. Controls and Compliance

  • Support adherence to internal underwriting guidelines, operational controls, treaty exclusions and relevant regulatory requirement by ensuring documentation and booking practices are compliant andaudit-ready.

5. Claims Support and Performance Tracking

  • Upon claims notification, summarize keydetails and share with the underwriters in the team for visibility and action, where required.
  • Coordinate and confirm performancereflection/booing items relating to claims (as applicable) for reporting and portfolio tracking.

6. Premium Collection Support and Reconciliation

  • Support Finance/Credit Control in liaisingwith intermediaries to follow up on outstanding premium collections.
  • Assist the Processing/Admin team to identify policy numbers for unidentified remittances, and support Credit Control in Reconciling received Premiums to the corresponding policies.


Technical Knowledge:

  • Basic understanding of marine insurance is preferred.
  • Candidates without marine insurance exposure should possess strong logistics operations knowledge, including hands-on experience with shipping documentation and processes (e.g. invoicing, Bills of Lading, shipping instructions, and related trade documents).
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office (especially Excel) is required.

Soft Skills:

  • Quick to grasp context and workflows, with a strong ability to summarize key points clearly.
  • Highly organized and detail-oriented, with strong documentation discipline
  • Strong numeracy with the ability to validate figures, spot discrepancies and ensure data accuracy.

districts.location: D01 Cecil, Marina, People’s Park, Raffles Place
